blogPosts.title: Leadership Begins With Self-Awareness
blogPosts.excerpt: A psychology-informed view of leadership that starts with attention, reflection, and personal responsibility.
blogPosts.author: Steve Muthusi, PhD
blogPosts.content: Leadership is often described through outcomes: influence, direction, performance, and public courage. Yet the inner life of a leader is where the work begins.

blogPosts.title: Mental Wellness in High Performance Environments
blogPosts.excerpt: Why excellence must be built with rest, reflection, and psychologically safe communities.
blogPosts.author: Steve Muthusi, PhD
blogPosts.content: High performance settings can reward speed while quietly neglecting restoration. Wellness is not the absence of pressure; it is the presence of habits and relationships that help people carry pressure wisely.

events.title: PDC Leadership Intensive
events.description: A focused leadership formation gathering for professionals, student leaders, and community builders.

galleryItems.title: Leadership cohort dialogue
galleryItems.description: PDC leaders discussing service, identity, and personal excellence.

comments.name: Grace
comments.message: This connects leadership to inner work in a very practical way.
